title: Issue #47 Plan: Read-Only Profile Inference Discovery

issue.title: M7.1 Add profile inference from DB metadata, sample JSON, XSD, sample XML

summary.goal: Add read-only profile-field generation from discovered metadata and sample/schema artifacts beyond the M2 explicit-contract path.
summary.approach: Introduce a pure inference layer and MCP wrapper that normalize DB metadata summaries, sample JSON, XSD, and sample XML into the existing issue #43 profile-field contracts for later profile and map builders.
summary.safety_contract: Discovery only. Do not create, update, or mutate Boomi components. Do not call credentials or construct a Boomi SDK client from the MCP wrapper.
non_goals: Do not change the issue #43 direct profile generation contract for explicit DB fields, JSON profile trees, or XML profile trees.
Do not create Boomi profile, map, connector, or process components during discovery.
Do not infer business field mappings from samples.
Do not generate payload templates, SQL, scripts, XSLT, lookup tables, or default business values from samples.
Do not require direct JDBC access from the MCP host for DB metadata discovery.
Do not add namespace-aware XML profile building in this issue.
Do not index arbitrary existing live Boomi profile XML for literal-UUID transform.map refs; that remains a separate deferred capability.

facts_used: Issue #43 helpers already generate builder-ready contracts for explicit DB read fields, JSON profile trees, and XML profile trees.
Existing returned contracts include generation_mode, component_type, profile_type, component_name, profile_config, field_index_by_path, and mappable_paths.
The four issue #47 modes are currently listed as deferred: profile_from_db_metadata, profile_from_sample_json, profile_from_xsd, and profile_from_sample_xml.
Existing logical path conventions are DB field name, JSON Root/list[]/key, and XML repeating element paths using [] on descendants.
JSONGeneratedProfileBuilder consumes the profile_from_json_schema contract.
JSON builder field indexes add key, key_path, and name_path later during XML emission/indexing.
Supported JSON leaf data types are character, number, datetime, and boolean.
XMLGeneratedProfileBuilder consumes the profile_from_xml_schema contract.
Current XML generated profile support is element-only.
Attributes, namespaces, namespace_uri, schema_import, and xsd keys are rejected by the M2 XML builder.
Supported XML leaf data types are character, number, datetime, and boolean, with boolean stored as character format in emitted profile XML.
Literal existing-profile UUID refs are currently rejected because they cannot be indexed in M2.
MAP_PROFILE_INDEX_UNAVAILABLE for literal UUIDs is about existing-profile schema discovery, not artifact inference.
Read-only tool envelopes use read_only=true, boomi_mutation=false, and raw_xml_exposed=false.
Existing review code resolves profile field indexes from generated in-spec profile components and treats literal existing-profile discovery as future work.
Structured errors use code, field, hint, and details fields.
Read-only MCP tools are registered with readOnlyHint annotations.
Tools that should not call Boomi avoid get_current_user, get_secret, and Boomi construction.
list_capabilities filters the static catalog against the live FastMCP registry.
get_schema_template and list_capabilities provide discoverability for tool contracts and workflows.
The profile.json inferred_from_sample_json note and profile.xml inferred_from_xsd/sample XML notes are artifact-inference notes that issue #47 should update to point at infer_profile_fields.
The transform.map existing_profile_index_discovery and MAP_PROFILE_INDEX_UNAVAILABLE notes are literal-existing-profile-index notes and should remain deferred after issue #47.
Capability catalog entries include category, description, read_only, no_boomi_mutation, parameters, and examples.
Discovery tools are read-only.
Discovery can produce profile-field contracts for LLM reasoning and later builders.
Discovery must not create SQL, payloads, mappings, or scripts automatically.
DB discovery should use Boomi-side execution or another safe mechanism; the MCP host should not require direct JDBC access.
M7 Discovery Tools includes infer_profile_fields for DB metadata, sample JSON, XSD, and sample XML.
M7 exit criteria require discovery tools not to mutate Boomi or customer systems.
M2 explicitly deferred metadata/sample/XSD/XML inference to issue #47.
Add read-only discovery/inference paths producing profile-field contracts for later builder use.
Supported modes are profile_from_db_metadata, profile_from_sample_json, profile_from_xsd, and profile_from_sample_xml.
Return structured field paths, inferred data types, confidence and ambiguity notes, and unsupported-shape errors.
Ambiguous sample-derived fields must be marked explicitly and require caller confirmation before apply.
XSD and sample XML inference must handle namespaces or fail with actionable unsupported-shape errors.
DB metadata discovery must not require direct JDBC from the MCP host unless a safe Boomi-side mechanism is explicitly implemented.

changes: Add src/boomi_mcp/categories/profile_inference.py as the public action layer for issue #47.
Implement infer_profile_fields_action(source_type, artifact, options=None) as a pure function over caller-supplied artifacts.
Accept artifact as a dict/list for structured metadata and parsed samples, or as a string for JSON, XSD, and XML sample content.
Normalize options from dict or JSON string; invalid options return a structured error envelope.
Add profile_from_db_metadata, profile_from_sample_json, profile_from_xsd, and profile_from_sample_xml to profile_generation.py or a sibling helper imported by profile_inference.py.
Reuse profile_from_db_read_fields, profile_from_json_schema, and profile_from_xml_schema after inference to keep builder contracts and path conventions stable.
Keep reject_unsupported_generation_source for unknown modes, but stop rejecting the four issue #47 modes.
Attach inference metadata to each field record without changing the existing profile_config shape consumed by builders.
Support caller-supplied summary keys columns, fields, or result_columns.
Require each column to provide a nonblank name and one of data_type, db_type, jdbc_type, or type.
Map common string types to character, numeric types to number, and date/time/timestamp types to datetime.
Treat boolean/bit and unknown non-binary types as ambiguous candidates that require caller confirmation.
Reject binary/blob/image/varbinary-like fields as unsupported because current DB read profile generation supports only character, number, and datetime.
Use nullable, required, mandatory, or optional metadata when available; otherwise default required to false with reduced confidence.
Reject duplicate or reserved-character field names through the existing profile_from_db_read_fields validation path.
Parse JSON string artifacts with json.loads; accept already-parsed dict/list artifacts directly.
Infer a profile.json root object for object samples.
For root arrays, support non-empty arrays of compatible objects by synthesizing a root object with one repeating child object; mark the array-derived structure as sample-derived.
Infer leaf types as character, number, datetime, or boolean using deterministic rules.
Mark mixed scalar types, null-only fields, optional fields across array entries, empty arrays, and heterogeneous arrays as ambiguous and confirmation_required.

Reject scalar-root samples, arrays of scalars, arrays with incompatible object/scalar mixing, invalid JSON, and unsupported nested array shapes with PROFILE_INFERENCE_UNSUPPORTED_SHAPE or PROFILE_INFERENCE_INVALID_SAMPLE.
Do not echo sample values into the returned contract; retain only paths, types, confidence, and ambiguity notes.
Use xml.etree.ElementTree from the standard library; do not add a new dependency.
Support a conservative XSD subset: inline or same-document xs:element, xs:complexType, xs:sequence, xs:simpleType restriction bases, minOccurs, maxOccurs, and maxOccurs='unbounded'.
Map XSD string-like types to character, decimal/integer/float/double types to number, date/time/dateTime types to datetime, and boolean to boolean.

Reject or mark unsupported: xs:choice, xs:all, xs:any, attributes, mixed content, imports/includes, external schema locations, complexContent/extension/restriction, list/union, substitution groups, and recursive type references.
Reject target namespaces or qualified element namespaces with PROFILE_INFERENCE_UNSUPPORTED_NAMESPACE unless the shape can be represented losslessly by the current namespace-less XML builder.
Return actionable details for unsupported namespace/schema constructs, including the unsupported construct and path where available.
Parse XML samples with ElementTree using a safe parser setup and reject DOCTYPE/entities before parsing.
Infer profile.xml element trees from nested elements only.
Infer repeated sibling elements as max_occurs=-1 and optional children across repeated parents as required=false/min_occurs=0.
Infer leaf data types from text values without echoing the text values.

Reject attributes, mixed content, comments/processing instructions that affect shape, namespaced element tags, invalid XML, and recursive same-name ancestor patterns with structured unsupported-shape errors.
Use profile_from_xml_schema to validate the final inferred tree and produce stable field_index_by_path and mappable_paths.
Add default limits max_input_chars=200000, max_nodes=1000, and max_fields=500.
Allow options to lower limits and to raise them only within hard-coded safe caps.
For oversized inputs, return PROFILE_INFERENCE_INPUT_TOO_LARGE with truncation metadata and ready_for_builder=false.
Never include truncated raw artifacts or sample values in the output.
Import infer_profile_fields_action in server.py and register infer_profile_fields as a read-only MCP tool.
The wrapper must parse options if passed as JSON string and return safety flags on parse errors.
The wrapper must not accept a profile parameter and must not call get_current_user, get_secret, or Boomi.
Add infer_profile_fields to list_capabilities_action under Integration Authoring or Discovery.
Add get_schema_template coverage for resource_type='profile_inference' or resource_type='component' discovery protocol entries documenting modes, inputs, outputs, safety flags, and examples.

Rewrite only the artifact-inference out_of_scope notes in meta_tools.py: profile.json inferred_from_sample_json should point at profile_from_sample_json, and profile.xml inferred_from_xsd/sample XML should point at profile_from_xsd and profile_from_sample_xml.

Leave transform.map existing_profile_index_discovery and MAP_PROFILE_INDEX_UNAVAILABLE notes deferred; clarify where useful that infer_profile_fields does not index arbitrary existing live Boomi profile XML for literal-UUID map refs.

mcp_tool.name: infer_profile_fields
mcp_tool.signature: infer_profile_fields(source_type: str, artifact: Any, options: dict | str | None = None) -> dict
mcp_tool.mutation_behavior: No Boomi API calls, no credentials lookup, no component creation or update.

options.component_name: Optional display name copied into the generated contract.
options.root_name: Optional root name for JSON sample inference when a synthetic root is needed.
options.array_item_name: Optional name for synthetic array item objects when a root array is inferred.
options.max_input_chars: Optional input character limit, clamped to implementation hard caps.
options.max_nodes: Optional parsed-node limit, clamped to implementation hard caps.
options.max_fields: Optional inferred-field limit, clamped to implementation hard caps.
options.datetime_detection: Optional boolean, default true, controlling conservative ISO-like datetime recognition in samples.

structured_errors.when: source_type is missing/unknown, artifact has the wrong container type, options cannot be parsed, or required metadata keys are absent.
JSON, XML, or XSD content is syntactically invalid or cannot be parsed safely.
The artifact is valid but contains a shape outside the supported inference subset, such as scalar JSON root, XML attributes, XSD choice, or binary DB fields.
Inference can produce candidate fields but one or more fields have mixed, null-only, unknown, or sample-limited types that require confirmation.
The raw artifact, parsed node count, or inferred field count exceeds configured or hard-coded limits.
XSD or XML uses namespace semantics that cannot be represented by the current namespace-less generated XML profile builder.
XSD type references or XML sample shape indicate recursive/nested self-reference that cannot be represented safely.

db_metadata_tests: Happy path maps varchar/int/timestamp metadata to character/number/datetime fields.
columns, fields, and result_columns aliases are accepted.
nullable=false or required=true marks required fields.
Unknown nullable metadata defaults required=false and lowers confidence.
Boolean/bit types are returned as ambiguous candidates requiring confirmation.
Duplicate names and reserved path characters propagate existing profile generation errors.
No direct JDBC, Boomi SDK, or credentials calls are made.
json_sample_tests: Nested object sample returns profile.json contract with deterministic paths.
Array of homogeneous objects infers repeating paths using [] and required=false for fields missing in some rows.
String, number, boolean, and ISO-like datetime values infer supported data types.
Mixed scalar types mark field ambiguity and ready_for_builder=false.
Empty arrays, scalar arrays, scalar root, heterogeneous object/scalar arrays, and invalid JSON return structured errors.
Sample values are not echoed in the serialized output.
Oversized input returns truncation metadata and ready_for_builder=false.
xsd_tests: Simple sequence XSD returns profile.xml contract with min_occurs and max_occurs.
Nested and unbounded elements produce expected [] logical paths.
Built-in string, numeric, boolean, and date/time XSD types map correctly.
Attributes, choice, all, any, import/include, extension, mixed content, and unsupported binary types return actionable unsupported-shape errors.
xml_sample_tests: Nested element-only sample returns profile.xml contract.
Repeated sibling elements infer max_occurs=-1 and descendant [] paths.
Optional children across repeated parents lower required/min_occurs.
Leaf text infers character, number, datetime, and boolean where unambiguous.
Attributes, mixed content, namespaced tags, recursive same-name ancestor patterns, invalid XML, and DOCTYPE/entities return structured errors.
Sample text values are not echoed in the output.
mcp_wrapper_tests: infer_profile_fields is registered with FastMCP and appears in list_capabilities when registered.
Every success and error response carries read_only=true, boomi_mutation=false, and raw_xml_exposed=false.
Wrapper accepts options as dict or JSON string.
Wrapper rejects invalid options JSON with PROFILE_INFERENCE_INVALID_INPUT.
Patch get_current_user, get_secret, and Boomi to assert they are never called.
Repeated calls with the same artifact produce stable structured output.
schema_template_tests: profile.json inferred_from_sample_json now points at infer_profile_fields profile_from_sample_json.
profile.xml inferred_from_xsd/sample XML now points at infer_profile_fields profile_from_xsd and profile_from_sample_xml.
transform.map existing_profile_index_discovery notes remain deferred and do not claim infer_profile_fields supports literal existing-profile UUID refs.
regression_tests: Existing issue #43 profile_from_db_read_fields tests still pass.
Existing issue #43 profile_from_json_schema tests still pass.
Existing issue #43 profile_from_xml_schema tests still pass.
Unknown generation mode still raises UNSUPPORTED_PROFILE_GENERATION_SOURCE.
database_to_api_sync archetype output remains unchanged unless explicitly updated to reference infer_profile_fields in documentation metadata.

acceptance_criteria: Discovery emits a profile-field contract suitable for existing profile and map builders without creating Boomi components.
Ambiguous sample-derived fields are marked explicitly with confirmation_required=true and ready_for_builder=false.
XSD and sample XML inference either handles the supported namespace-less subset or fails with actionable unsupported-shape errors.
DB metadata discovery operates only on caller-supplied metadata summaries and does not require direct JDBC access from the MCP host.
The MCP wrapper is provably read-only in tests and returns stable structured output.
Negative tests cover ambiguous arrays, mixed types, recursive XML, unsupported namespace patterns, invalid samples, and oversized-input truncation.
No inference path invents business mappings or payload templates from samples.
Only artifact-inference schema-template notes are rewritten to infer_profile_fields; existing live-profile indexing notes remain deferred.
assumptions: The public tool name should be infer_profile_fields, matching docs/MCP_TOOL_DESIGN.md and the M7 roadmap.
Namespace-aware generated XML profile emission is out of scope; unsupported namespace cases fail rather than silently stripping namespaces.
The initial implementation should use only Python standard library parsers for JSON and XML/XSD.
Confidence can be represented as a small enum string such as high, medium, low, or ambiguous.
Builder-ready means structurally compatible with profile_config and field_index_by_path conventions, not immediately safe to apply when ready_for_builder=false.
Issue #47 does not solve literal existing-profile UUID indexing for transform.map; it only creates profile-field contracts from supplied metadata/sample/schema artifacts.
The three transform-map existing_profile_index_discovery or MAP_PROFILE_INDEX_UNAVAILABLE blocks in meta_tools.py should remain deferred after issue #47.
